source_title: BALI: A Tropical Paradise | Full Travel Guide 2026 | Bali travel in 4k
raw_text: [Music] When you step off the plane, the first thing you notice is the smell of incense in the air. Warm wind dances through the palm trees and the sound of a distant gameland welcomes you to a land that feels both sacred and wild. This is not just a trip. This is Bali. But here's the secret. While it's known for luxury resorts and celebrity retreats, Bali can be experienced fully even on a small budget. In this video, I'll take you on a cinematic journey through Bali's lush jungles, serene beaches, spiritual temples, and a travel hack that cuts your hotel cost by 75%. Bali's international airport in Dempasar is modern, efficient, and for most nationalities. You get a visa-free stay for up to 30 days. As you exit the airport, you can grab a local SIM card for $5 and book a ride using the Grab app, Bali's version of Uber. Start your journey in Uluatu in southern Bali, home to some of the island's best beaches and epic cliffside views. [Music] Renting a scooter costs around $5 per day and it gives you full freedom. Visit Padang Padang Beach made famous by the movie Eat Pray Love. [Music] For the perfect sunset, head to Ulu Watu Temple perched on the edge of a dramatic cliff. Stick around for the traditional Kekak fire dance at sunset. Just $4 for a ticket. Luxury resorts like Alila Villas cost upwards of $500 per night, but cozy guest houses start at just $25. Next, head north to Ubud, the spiritual center of the island. The drive takes about 2 hours. You'll pass through rice terraces, jungle roads, and sleepy villages. In Ubud, explore the Tagalang rice terraces, a breathtaking landscape that feels like a painting. Entry is around $1. Take a yoga class at the yoga barn. Try a $3 smoothie bowl and relax in a jungle view calf. Don't miss the sacred monkey forest where over 1,000 longtailed macocks roam freely. For something less touristy, explore the east coast. Stop by Terta Ganga, a royal water palace with koi filled pools and stone carvings. Entry is just $2. Visit Virgin Beach, a hidden white sand paradise with crystalclear waters. You can get fresh grilled fish at a beach shack for just $5. From Sonorport, take a 45minut speedboat to Nusupanita, a rugged, untouched island. Here you'll find Kelling King Beach, where the cliffs form the shape of a T-Rex. The hike down is steep, but the view unreal. Swim with manta rays at Manta Point or snorkel in vibrant coral gardens for about $30. Renting a scooter on Nusa Panita costs $7 a day, but roads are rough, so drive carefully. End your journey in Kang, Bali's trendy beach town. It's full of hip cafes, surf schools, and boutique shops. Take a surf lesson for $20. Sip coffee at beachfront cafes and enjoy the Bali sunset at Echo Beach. Luxury villas here go for $100 to $300 per night. But you can find great Airbnbs starting from $40. Transport scooter $5 per day. Taxes $10 to 15 per ride. Food. Street food starts at $2. Restaurants average $8 to $15. Stay budget $20 to $50. Mid-range $80 to $150. Luxury $300 plus. [Music] Budget travelers $40 to $80 per day. Mid-range $120 to $250. Luxury $400 plus. Flights from India start at $250 round trip. Europe or US $600 to $12. Best time to visit April to June and September to early November for fewer crowds, lush greenery, and lower prices. Avoid December to February if you don't like rain. Bali is not just a destination. It's a transformation. A place where you reconnect with nature, with people, with yourself. Whether you're walking barefoot on the beach, meditating in a jungle hut, or sipping coconut under a temple tree, this island changes you. Welcome to Bali. [Music]

source_title: Hidden  Bali 12 Secret PlacesYou Didn't Know Exist
raw_text: Welcome to the island of the gods, Bali, Indonesia. Known for its beautiful beaches and party towns. But what if we told you there's another side to Bali? One that most tourists miss completely. In this video, we're uncovering 12 of Bali's most secret, untouched, and soul stirring destinations. From secluded waterfalls to mystical temples, this is the Bali you've never seen before. Get ready to explore and don't forget to subscribe for more hidden travel treasures. One, Tuc Waterfall. First on our list is the magical Tucfall. Located inside a cave, this hidden wonder feels like a portal to another world. Early morning light rays stream through the cave's opening, illuminating the falls in a surreal glow. Two, Sidman Valley. Want to escape the crowds and connect with Bali's true spirit? Sideman Valley is a slice of rural paradise. Lush rice fields, gentle rivers, and the towering Mount Aong in the background make it perfect for hiking, cycling, or just slowing down. Three, Leak Leak Waterfall. Next up is Leak Leak Waterfall. A narrow stream tucked inside dense jungle with a wooden bamboo bridge and tropical plants all around. It's a fairy tale come to life. Four. Panggalipuran village. Panggalipuran is one of the cleanest and most wellpreserved traditional villages in the world. Here cultural heritage is still alive and every path is lined with manicured gardens and ornate gates. It's a living museum. Five. And Lipa beaches. On the eastern coast lies and a peaceful alternative to Bali's crowded beach towns. Black volcanic sand, coralrich waters, and sunrises that light up Mount Aong make this a divers's paradise. Six. Jatilui rice terraces. A UNESCO World Heritage site, the Jatilui Rice Terraces offer the most spectacular rice field views on the island. Spanning miles, this ancient irrigation system is a masterpiece of Balines agriculture. Seven. Hidden Canyon Beiji Gowang. Ready for adventure? Head to Beijing Gawang, a hidden canyon carved by river water over thousands of years. Climb, wave, and swim through narrow passages surrounded by moss covered walls. Eight, Nusa Senan. While most people visit Nusupanita, nearby Nusa Senan is a lesserknown island that's equally stunning. Blue lagoon is one of the bluest bays in the world. Cliff jumping, quiet cafes and crystal clear water await. Nine. Goa Gajaja Elephant Cave. Goa Gajaja or the Elephant Cave is one of Bali's most spiritual spots. This ancient Hindu site has stone carvings, meditation caves, and bathing pools believed to purify your soul. 10. Banyala Twin Waterfalls. Tucked away in North Bali. Banyamala offers two waterfalls cascading side by side into a pristine pool. Surrounded by vines and rocks, it's a peaceful swim you won't forget. 11. Campuhan Ridge Walk. A scenic trail that begins in Yubid. Campuhan Ridge is a lush path with sweeping views of jungle valleys. Ideal for a sunrise walk or golden hour jog. 12. Tamman Tieranga. A former royal palace. Tier Ganga is a water garden with sacred pools, koi filled fountains, and intricate stone statues. Walking its stepping stones feels like dancing across water. And there you have it. 12 of Bali's bestkept secrets. Whether you're a first timer or returning traveler, ditch the tourist trail and find the real Bali. If you found this video helpful, hit that like button, subscribe to our channel, and let us know in the comments which hidden gem will you explore first.

source_title: Best Places To Visit in Bali in 2026 - Travel Guide
raw_text: Welcome to our video on the top 10 must-see destinations on the picturesque island of Bali. This Indonesian island, located at the westernmost end of the Lesser Sunda Islands, is home to various cultural hotspots, natural wonders, and popular tourist destinations. With Denpasar as its provincial capital and situated between Java and Lombok, Bali attracts millions of visitors annually. In this video, we'll take you on a journey to discover some of the best places to visit on the island. So, get comfortable and start planning your next adventure to Bali. Number 1 Ubud: Known as the cultural center of Bali, Ubud offers stunning rice terraces, traditional Balinese temples, and delicious local cuisine. The town is located in the island's central hills and is a great place to escape the hustle and bustle of the more touristy areas. Visitors can explore the many art galleries and museums, such as the Neka Art Museum, the Agung Rai Museum of Art, and the Museum Puri Lukisan, to gain insight into the island's rich artistic heritage. The Ubud Royal Palace and the Ubud Market are also must-see attractions where you can immerse yourself in the local culture and traditions. Take a walk through the market and find various traditional crafts and souvenirs. Number 2 Uluwatu:Uluwatu is one of the most popular surf destinations in all of Bali due to the making of Morning of the Earth, a 1971 classic surf film. If you are not a fan of surfing you can take a leisurely walk along the cliffs and admire the breathtaking ocean views. Its cliffside temple offers stunning views and traditional Balinese dances during sunset. The temple, situated at the southernmost point of the island, is revered as one of Bali's six most significant temples. The temple is believed to protect Bali against evil and is also home to a sizable population of monkeys inhabiting nearby forests. It's a unique experience to see them interacting with visitors, but keep your belongings safe, as they can be pretty cheeky. Number 3 Tanah Lot: Another coastal temple, Tanah Lot, is a popular spot for sunset and is said to be one of the most important temples in Bali. The temple is on a large rock formation surrounded by the ocean, making it a picturesque location. Visitors can also enjoy the beautiful surroundings by walking along the beach or watching traditional Balinese dances. The temple is also home to a beautiful and tranquil garden where visitors can explore the many statues and shrines. It's also said to be one of the most photographed temples in Bali. If you like golf visit the Nirwana Bali Golf Club designed by legendary pro golfer Greg Norman. Number 4 Kuta - A popular sun and beach tourist spot, Kuta offers excellent shopping, dining, and nightlife. For those looking for a more laid-back experience, Kuta also offers many yoga and meditation centers. Additionally, the beach is home to the famous Kuta beach break, one of Bali's most consistent surf breaks. Number 5 Seminyak: A step up from Kuta in terms of luxury, Seminyak offers high-end boutiques and resorts. The beach is a prime location for surfers of all abilities. Visitors can also take a leisurely walk along the beach promenade, checking out the various shops, bars, and restaurants. Kuta is also renowned for its vibrant nightlife scene, featuring many bars and clubs. Visitors can also explore the many art galleries, such as the W Hotel's Woobar and the Biasa Art Space, which feature works by some of Bali's most talented contemporary artists. Number 6 Nusa Dua - This area is known for its beautiful beaches and luxury resorts, making it the perfect spot for a relaxing vacation. Visitors can enjoy many water activities like snorkeling, diving, and sailing. The area is also home to the Bali Collection, an extensive shopping and dining complex that offers a variety of international and local brands. Nusa Dua is also home to the Bali National Golf Club, a must-visit for golf enthusiasts, which offers a challenging 18-hole course. Number 7 Jimbaran: This coastal town is famous for its seafood BBQs on the beach. Visitors can enjoy a delicious fresh seafood meal while watching the sunset over the ocean. The city is also known for its traditional fishing village atmosphere, where visitors can watch the local fishermen bring in their catch of the day. The city is also home to the beautiful Balangan Beach, a secluded spot that offers a peaceful retreat from the more touristy areas. Number 8 Canggu: A popular spot for surfers, Canggu also offers excellent cafes and restaurants. The area is also known for its laid-back vibe and bohemian atmosphere. Visitors can enjoy some of the best surf spots in Bali, such as Echo Beach and Batu Bolong Beach, take a yoga class or explore the many art galleries. Visitors can also explore the many rice fields and traditional villages scattered throughout the region. Number 9 Gili Islands: The Gili Islands, located just off the northwest coast of Lombok, offer crystal clear waters and excellent snorkeling and diving. Visitors can discover the diverse marine life and coral reefs while relishing in the island's relaxed atmosphere. The Gili Islands boast picturesque beaches like Gili Trawangan, Gili Air, and Gili Meno. Tourists can also visit a variety of small shops and restaurants that are dispersed throughout the islands. The Gili Islands are also a great place to learn how to scuba dive, as the waters are home to a diverse array of marine life and coral reefs. Number 10 Sanur: Known for its laid-back atmosphere, Sanur offers beautiful beaches and traditional markets. Visitors can walk along the beach promenade and explore the many shops, bars, and restaurants. The area is also home to many conventional Balinese temples and is a great place to learn about the island's culture. Sanur is also home to the Le Mayeur Museum, which showcases the works of Belgian painter Adrien-Jean Le Mayeur, who lived in Bali for many years. Visitors can also take a bike tour around the village and explore the many traditional markets and crafts centers. Bali is a paradise island that offers something for everyone. From stunning rice terraces, traditional Balinese temples, and delicious local cuisine to beautiful beaches, luxury resorts, and exciting nightlife. Remember to visit the temples, enjoy the delicious local cuisine and engage with the friendly local people. source_title: Bali Trip Cost and Itinerary From India 2025
raw_text: I spent 14 days in Bali and tracked every single rupee from flights to food, stays to experiences. Here's the full budget breakdown of what it truly costs to visit Bali in 2025. Let's begin. The best time to visit Bali is during the shoulder season which is between September and October. No rains, fewer crowds and less expensive. I also went in October 2024. In India, you get direct flights from Bangalore and Delhi only. But this is very expensive. A round trip of Rs 50,000-55,000. So the better option is to go via Singapore Airlines and Malaysian Airlines. Here, you'll get round trip tickets for Rs 35,000. All these flights are full service airlines. So you'll get food, drinks, blankets, everything. There will be a layover of 4-5 hours. But when I landed at Changi Airport, I didn't even realize where the time went to see the transit and the airport. So it's better to save that money. Don't get into a direct flight. Whenever you're planning to go, book your flights 2-3 months in advance. Look at the sky scanner, look at the dates where you're getting the best deals and rates. And then book it. Next, visa and entry requirements. For Indians, there's a visa on arrival in Indonesia. So you can go and get a visa. But the queues are very long. So that's why you should get an e-visa from their official website. The cost is Rs 2,800. Along with that, you'll have to pay an eco-tax of Rs 850 to preserve their country. You can either pay it online or at the airport. I did both online and I saved a lot of time. Next, sim card. I made a huge mistake here. I bought a local sim card from the airport. I paid Rs 2,500 for a 30-day plan. If I had bought it from a local shop outside the airport, I would have got the same thing for Rs 600. So please guys, don't buy sim cards from the airport. Buy it outside. You will save up on a lot of money. Next, currency. 1 Indian Rupee is equal to 187 Indonesian Rupee. Don't assume that Indonesia is cheap after hearing this conversion. There, the price of everything is in lakhs and thousands. I used to get very confused. But I used this simple trick. Divide everything by 200 to get an estimate of the Indian cost. If you're traveling internationally, it's important to have a good card along with cash. In Bali, most restaurants, supermarkets, and cabs accept cards. But if you only depend on cash, you will have constant anxiety about cash loss and fake notes. In Bali mainland, there is a lot of problem with fake notes. In many money exchange centers, they will put one or two fake notes in between your 10 notes. And they are so swift with their hands that you won't even notice. So a good travel card can save you from this fear. I personally prefer the SCAPIA Federal Credit Card for these three features. First of all, this card has Zero Forex Markup. That means you don't have to pay any extra for international transactions. Most credit cards charge 2-4% extra for every international transaction. Which can be a huge amount in a 14-day trip. The second feature is their Smart Forex feature. With this, you will be able to track all your currency transactions at the same place. This feature tells you how much you have spent in which currency. With this, you can manage your money well. And the third feature is my favorite. With SCAPIA's credit card, you will get airport privileges in 76 domestic airports. Let's say you are hungry and you don't want to wait in a long line at the lounge. You can go to an outlet and get a free meal right away. Along with this, if you want to shop, eat, or go to a spa at the airport, you can instantly get Rs. 1000 off. With this credit card. This amount varies from airport to airport but this is a great feature. So if you want to make your travel budget with your day-to-day expenses, check out the SCAPIA Federal Credit Card. The link is in my description. After all these details, now it was time to leave the Denpasar airport and start my trip. After leaving the airport, we took a cab to our hotel using the Grab app. Whenever you go to Indonesia, download Grab and Gojek. These are all-in-one apps. Cabs, food, groceries, everything will be available with this. The hotel was just 20 minutes away from the hotel. We took this for just one night because we had to travel the next day. The tariff was Rs. 6500. The room was amazing for its price. There was a bathtub in the room but we didn't use it. The next morning, we went to a local warung for breakfast. If you eat non-vegetarian, you will save a lot of money in Bali because these warungs give you authentic Indonesian food for Rs. 250-300 per meal. After breakfast, we left for our first destination, Munduk. Munduk is 80-90 km away from Bali's mainland. It costed us around Rs. 700,000 IDR to reach this hill station in North Bali. You will find a lot of coffee plantations on the way to Munduk. We slowly explored and reached Munduk in the evening. If you want to meet the best people in Bali, please go to Munduk. Not many tourists have come to Munduk yet. It is not overcrowded. And it will give you the best that nature has to offer. But there are no money exchange shops in Munduk. Even the ATMs are closed in the evening. So, please take your currency with you. After reaching Munduk in the evening, we hired a local guide. His name was Budi. Budi took us to a restaurant nearby. We had a nice and cute dinner there. Then, we went to sleep early because the next day was the real Munduk Darshan. At 5.30 am, Budi picked us up from our hotel and took us to our first spot. Two rivers were merging at this spot. And we saw the sun rising just above it. It was one of the most beautiful sunrises that I have ever experienced in my life. It was a little chilly. There was not much traffic. So, you could hear all the sounds of nature. After breakfast, we started our jungle trek. Budi dropped us to our jungle guide. His name was Ketut. Ketut told us the story of Ramayana and Shivji in Indonesia while trekking for the next one hour. It was an amazing experience. Bali's guides are amazing storytellers and even better photographers. The jungle trek was not that intense. It was one hour long. Anyone can do it. And it ended with a beautiful boat ride to the other side of the jungle. Then, Budi took us to Lemuki waterfalls. The natural water slides that you have seen in Bali on Instagram are actually Lemuki waterfalls. To reach this waterfall, it is an intense trek of 2-3 hours. But the reward is insane. A natural water slide followed by a home-cooked Indonesian meal. This waterfall trek was around 4-5 hours long. And it cost us around 4,500 INR. Around 2,500 INR. The next morning, Budi took us to the harbour. And from there, we took a ferry to our next destination, Nusa Penida. This ferry was an adventure ride. The waves were jumpy. There was an Indian family beside me whose kids were vomiting. Mom was crying. And the uncles were scolding their kids. I was also feeling a little dizzy in the midst of all this chaos. But finally, the ferry landed. We safely reached Nusa Penida's harbour. After this roller coaster ride, we took a cab from the harbour to our hotel. Our hotel was just 10 minutes away. And this hotel was my first glamping experience. The experience of staying in a tent in the middle of the jungle was very fresh. You wake up to the sounds of nature. Totally worth it. It cost us around 20,000 INR for 2 nights. That means 5,000 INR per person per night. As soon as we reached the hotel, we rented a scooter which cost us around 1,000 INR a day. We left the scooter to get a massage by the sea. Honestly, before Indonesia, I was not a massage person. But Balinese massage opened my eyes. They will give you a 90-minute full-body massage for just 1,500 INR. And after the massage, your body feels so relaxed and relaxed that I took a massage every other day during this 14-day trip. And it was totally worth it. So whenever you go to Indonesia, please take a massage experience. Enjoy it. You won't get such massages in this price in India. After the massage, we went to a sunset beach club for dinner. It cost us around 2,000 INR per person. And it was honestly very underwhelming. We blindly trusted Google reviews. And after this experience, we relied on local recommendations for the entire trip. So please, guys, Google reviews only recommend hyped-up restaurants. So please, don't blindly trust them. Then we went to Bali's most famous spot, Kelingking Beach. The famous T-Rex-shaped cliff in front of which people click their photos. We also thought of going down and exploring everything. But there was no use. Currents were so high that you cannot swim there. And the people who were coming up from below said that the trek is so difficult that you should not go at all. So we also clicked a photo like the rest of the people. And we left from there within 15-20 minutes. The problem was that the distance between all these spots was around 1-1.5 hours. So when you have to spend 15 minutes in each spot, then the entire experience feels overrated. And that's what happened with me. I would never ever visit Nusa Penida again. Because it is optimized for just Instagram posts. There is nothing worth seeing or experiencing there. Next day, it was time to leave Nusa and reach Kangu. Because there was my friend's bachelor's trip with our entire boys gang. If you have a big group and you are planning a vacation, then Bali is the perfect destination. The villas here are so grand that it leaves Goa behind. Huge Bedrooms, Great Common Areas, Infinity Pools and 24x7 Staff You won't get a better service than this And the best part, the villas here are way cheaper than Goa We were 12 people and we stayed in this villa for 3 days The total cost was 2.5 Lakh Indian Rupees Which means 7000 Rupees per person per night And this wasn't an ordinary villa This was actually a resort with 3 mini villas Right next to the beach with an infinity pool and a 24x7 staff You won't get a service like this in India at this cost There was only one agenda of these days, Party We used to go to different clubs every evening Like Fins, Sawaya, Mexicola If you like clubbing, if you like to go out then these are great spots It costed me around 10-12,000 Rupees for these 3 nights Also because my friend who was getting married You know pitched in a bigger amount So generally these places are not that cheap It will cost you a little more But then it also depends on how much you consume We used to have fun in the evening and go on different adventures in the morning Like ATV rides If there is such a big group, then the fun of ATV rides is different It's not a normal jungle ATV ride Everyone is hitting each other, everyone is sledging each other It's just a different boys experience It costed me around 3000 Rupees for this ride And it was totally worth it In these 3 days, my food expenses were a little high Because all my other friends were vegetarians And in these local war rooms, you will get very less vegetarian options So we used to go out to Indian restaurants and burger places And here your cost of every meal will be around 1500 Rupees For these 3 days of travel, we had booked a Tempo Traveler We are with Vibes It costed us 20,000 Rupees for 3 days It was a very convenient and cheap option for such a big group It was a perfect bachelor's trip in these 3 days We enjoyed a lot with our friend Rest of the people left And only me and my friend were left Who was about to get married And then we headed to my favorite destination in the entire world Gili Travangan I feel that if a good person has made a documentary on me Then it will have 2 phases Before Gili and after Gili Before coming to Gili Travangan I was a hardcore city person who was consumed by the idea of consuming I used to feel that for happiness in life Consumption is very important Stimulation is very important But after going to this place, I have changed Whenever I meet someone One topic of conversation is always my experience about Gili Travangan Gili Ti is a donut shaped island Where cars, scooters or any vehicle running on fuel is not allowed So no air pollution, no traffic, no noise pollution You will only see cycles and tongas As soon as we reached Gili Ti We rented a cycle for 80,000 INR Put our luggage in the tonga And we started riding our cycle Those 3-4 days were the best days of my life I had to do nothing to have fun The best thing about Gili Ti is that you don't have to go anywhere to enjoy This whole donut shaped island ends at a distance of 4-5 km So you just have to keep cycling And you can sit wherever you feel like While cycling, we saw a waiter with a surfboard We had a conversation with him And he agreed to teach us surfing And without doing anything, without planning anything We did our first surf lesson And that is the beauty of Gili Ti If you want to enjoy sunset in any other destination Then you have to go to a spot In Gili Ti, there are different types of stores, restaurants, bars on the left But on the right, it is an uninterrupted view to nature So whenever you feel that the sun is about to set You just have to park your cycle and sit And you don't even have to spend You don't have to necessarily go to a restaurant Which is now available in Goa If you want to enjoy sunset in Goa Then you might have to go to a restaurant This is not there in Gili Ti Gili is also famous for its clubbing and party scene Because a lot of Australians come here And Australians love to party But my experience of Gili Ti was very different For the first time in my life I realized that to enjoy life You don't need a stimulant You just need nature, physical activity And a good company In Gili Ti, our stay cost was also negligible We stayed in a very good hostel And it costed us 800 rupees per night All in all, Gili Ti was the best part of my trip And after spending 3 days here Only one thought came to my mind Can't wait to come back The next day, I came back to Bali mainland from Gili Because I had a flight the next day I stayed in Ramada which was near the airport It was a standard experience Good room, good breakfast And it costed me 5000 rupees for the night This is the total cost breakdown of my entire trip This was not a budget trip by any means But we spent our money very wisely We didn't take any branded tour We always hired local guides Our accommodations were not 5 star or Instagram hyped places We searched, we did our research And then got the best value for our money If you want a luxury vacation in a good budget Then Bali is your answer Today, Bali's accommodations are cheaper than Goa So please explore Indonesia Indonesia has a lot to offer in terms of nature, adventure My trip personally was a good mix between adventure and clubbing You should also look for your mix And please, please save money and experience new countries This is Anshuman Sharma, that's all for today's video This is a very new video in our channel I have generally not shared much about my personal life So if you liked anything in this video Please, please comment I will be motivated by your comments and support So that I can bring more videos like this Goodbye and see you guys next time
